ddls:
CREATE TABLE "companies" (
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each company */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Name of the company */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry or sector the company operates in */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the company */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the company */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the company */,
  "website" TEXT /* Website of the company */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("company_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user account */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address associated with the user account */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "contacts" (
  "contact_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each contact */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the contact */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the contact */,
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the Companies table */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the contact */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the contact */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the contact */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("contact_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "contact_roles" (
  "contact_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the Contacts table */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the Roles table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("contact_id", "role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "contact_departments" (
  "contact_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the Contacts table */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the Departments table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("contact_id", "department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the Users table */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the Permissions table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "permission_id")
)
column_names: Industry or sector the company operates in
Email address associated with the user account
Foreign key referencing the Companies table
Foreign key referencing the Contacts table
Foreign key referencing the Roles table

Foreign key referencing the Departments table
